message.content:
This bibliography (spanning pages 44–47) functions as a comprehensive reference index for *The Global State of Game Publishing and Marketing 2025 Industry Report*, enabling retrieval of information across the following dimensions:

**Topics:** Video game publishing models (self-publishing, traditional publishing, Publisher-as-a-Service, post-publishing era), distribution evolution, cross-platform development and play, influencer marketing and brand collaborations, community building and user-generated content (UGC), gaming safety, console subscription models and monetization strategies, indie game funding, development costs, and distribution platforms, co-development, data analytics, esports market expansion (viewership, sponsorships, and KSA focus), game-to-film adaptations, digital vs. physical revenue distribution, and console market trends.

**Entities:** 
- *Research & Consulting Firms:* Bain & Company, Deloitte, Statista, Cognitive Market Research, EOS intelligence, Newzoo, PWC, CTA, Simon-Kucher, Konvoy, Market.us.
- *Media & Platforms:* Pocket Gamer.biz, PC Games Insider, Game Developer, Games Industry, Venture Beat, 80LV/80.lv, Gamalytic, Wired, GRYNOW, TapNation, Sportspro, BBC, LinkedIn, Kofluence, Lyncconf, Juego Studio, Main Leaf, Moldstud, Socially Powerful, TweakTown, Entertainment Weekly, Servers, Game Influencer.
- *Agencies, Studios & Companies:* Active Fence, Amber Studio, Neonhive, Cominted Labs, AAA Agency, Fourth Floor Creative, Remedy, Ready Player Me, Subscription Flow, Premortem Games/Popagenda.
- *Titles & Events:* Palworld, 2024 Esports World Cup, League of Legends (2024 World Championship), Helldivers 2.
- *Authors/Researchers:* Harvey NewMan, Mine Okur, Jarrod Palmer, Danielle Riendeau, Nick Romano, Agostino Simonetta, Derek Strickland, Dean Takahashi.

**Dates:** Source publication dates range from June 15, 2012, to January 16, 2025, with a heavy concentration between 2020 and 2025 (particularly 2024). Key referenced data years include 2023 and 2024.

**Definitions & Procedures:** The bibliography itself does not contain direct definitions or procedural steps, but it cites external sources that detail community-building guides, cross-platform development frameworks, influencer collaboration models, monetization pathways, gaming subscription structures, distinctions between mobile developers and publishers, and self-publishing/Publisher-as-a-Service business models.

**Claims & Metrics:** References sources containing specific industry claims and quantitative data, including:
- Palworld exceeding 25 million players and generating ~$500M in gross revenue.
- Helldivers 2 surpassing $200M in gross revenue.
- The indie games market achieving unprecedented success in 2024, alongside 2025 development cost estimates.
- 27 brands sponsoring the 2024 Esports World Cup.
- The 2024 League of Legends World Championship setting an all-time viewership record (~1.73M average).
- Digital formats accounting for 95% of video game revenues in 2023 (~$174.5 billion).
- 61% of U.S. gamers playing across multiple devices.
- Saudi Arabia (KSA) emerging as a significant player in the global esports market.
- TIGA and industry analysts positioning self-publishing as the "future of gaming" that enables greater commercial strategic thinking.
- Additional claims identify influencer marketing as a critical growth driver, cross-platform play as a dominant industry trend, and UGC as a primary driver of community engagement.

**Evidence Types:** Market research reports, statistical databases, journalistic articles, industry survey data, company whitepapers/blogs, academic/investigative publications, and consulting firm analyses.

**Tables & Figures:** Contains no tables or figures; serves exclusively as a citation list. (Note: One extracted segment contains only a placeholder "Visit Site" with no substantive reference data.)

This bibliography (spanning pages 44–47) functions as a comprehensive reference index for *The Global State of Game Publishing and Marketing 2025 Industry Report*, enabling retrieval of information across the following dimensions:

**Topics:** Video game publishing models (self-publishing, traditional publishing, Publisher-as-a-Service, post-publishing era), distribution evolution, cross-platform development and play, influencer marketing and brand collaborations, community building and user-generated content (UGC), gaming safety, console subscription models and monetization strategies, indie game funding, development costs, and distribution platforms, co-development, data analytics, esports market expansion (viewership, sponsorships, and KSA focus), game-to-film adaptations, digital vs. physical revenue distribution, and console market trends.

**Entities:** 
- *Research & Consulting Firms:* Bain & Company, Deloitte, Statista, Cognitive Market Research, EOS intelligence, Newzoo, PWC, CTA, Simon-Kucher, Konvoy, Market.us.
- *Media & Platforms:* Pocket Gamer.biz, PC Games Insider, Game Developer, Games Industry, Venture Beat, 80LV/80.lv, Gamalytic, Wired, GRYNOW, TapNation, Sportspro, BBC, LinkedIn, Kofluence, Lyncconf, Juego Studio, Main Leaf, Moldstud, Socially Powerful, TweakTown, Entertainment Weekly, Servers, Game Influencer.
- *Agencies, Studios & Companies:* Active Fence, Amber Studio, Neonhive, Cominted Labs, AAA Agency, Fourth Floor Creative, Remedy, Ready Player Me, Subscription Flow, Premortem Games/Popagenda.
- *Titles & Events:* Palworld, 2024 Esports World Cup, League of Legends (2024 World Championship), Helldivers 2.
- *Authors/Researchers:* Harvey NewMan, Mine Okur, Jarrod Palmer, Danielle Riendeau, Nick Romano, Agostino Simonetta, Derek Strickland, Dean Takahashi.

**Dates:** Source publication dates range from June 15, 2012, to January 16, 2025, with a heavy concentration between 2020 and 2025 (particularly 2024). Key referenced data years include 2023 and 2024.

**Definitions & Procedures:** The bibliography itself does not contain direct definitions or procedural steps, but it cites external sources that detail community-building guides, cross-platform development frameworks, influencer collaboration models, monetization pathways, gaming subscription structures, distinctions between mobile developers and publishers, and self-publishing/Publisher-as-a-Service business models.

**Claims & Metrics:** References sources containing specific industry claims and quantitative data, including:
- Palworld exceeding 25 million players and generating ~$500M in gross revenue.
- Helldivers 2 surpassing $200M in gross revenue.
- The indie games market achieving unprecedented success in 2024, alongside 2025 development cost estimates.
- 27 brands sponsoring the 2024 Esports World Cup.
- The 2024 League of Legends World Championship setting an all-time viewership record (~1.73M average).
- Digital formats accounting for 95% of video game revenues in 2023 (~$174.5 billion).
- 61% of U.S. gamers playing across multiple devices.
- Saudi Arabia (KSA) emerging as a significant player in the global esports market.
- TIGA and industry analysts positioning self-publishing as the "future of gaming" that enables greater commercial strategic thinking.
- Additional claims identify influencer marketing as a critical growth driver, cross-platform play as a dominant industry trend, and UGC as a primary driver of community engagement.

**Evidence Types:** Market research reports, statistical databases, journalistic articles, industry survey data, company whitepapers/blogs, academic/investigative publications, and consulting firm analyses.

**Tables & Figures:** Contains no tables or figures; serves exclusively as a citation list. (Note: One extracted segment contains only a placeholder "Visit Site" with no substantive reference data.)
